title: Helper Robots in Our Homes
passage:
Helper Robots in Our Homes

Robots are very useful machines. Today, many people have small robots in their homes. These robots help people with their daily housework.

The most common home robot is the robotic vacuum cleaner. This is a small, round machine. It moves around the floor and cleans up dust. It has special sensors, so it does not hit walls, tables, or chairs. When the battery is low, the robot automatically goes back to its charger. Owners do not need to clean the floors every day because this robot does it for them.

Another popular machine is the smart speaker. Many people have a smart speaker in their living room or kitchen. You can talk to this robot. For example, you can say, 'Play some music,' or 'What is the weather today?' The speaker understands your voice and answers your questions. It can also connect to other things in the house. It can turn on the lights, turn off the television, or lock the doors.

In some countries like Japan, there are helper robots for elderly people. These robots are bigger than vacuum cleaners. They have arms and screens. They can carry heavy boxes, bring food and water, and remind older people to take their medicine. They can also call for help if someone falls down.

Children also love toy robots. These toys can walk, sing, and teach children simple English words. Some toy robots can even play soccer. They are great friends for kids.

In the future, we will have more robots in our lives. They will do many difficult or boring jobs for us. Some people think robots are dangerous, but most people think they are very helpful. Robots make our lives easier and more comfortable every day.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
questions.text: What is the most common home robot?
questions.explanation: The second paragraph states: 'The most common home robot is the robotic vacuum cleaner.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
questions.text: What does the robotic vacuum cleaner do when its battery is low?

options.B: It goes back to its charger.

questions.explanation: The second paragraph states: 'When the battery is low, the robot automatically goes back to its charger.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
questions.text: What can a smart speaker do in the house?

options.A: It can turn on the lights.

questions.explanation: The third paragraph states: 'It can turn on the lights, turn off the television, or lock the doors.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
questions.text: Where can you find helper robots for elderly people?
questions.explanation: The fourth paragraph states: 'In some countries like Japan, there are helper robots for elderly people.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Robotic vacuum cleaners hit walls and chairs very often.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: The passage says: 'It has special sensors, so it does not hit walls, tables, or chairs.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Smart speakers can understand human voices.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: The passage says: 'The speaker understands your voice and answers your questions.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Helper robots for elderly people are smaller than vacuum cleaners.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: The passage says: 'These robots are bigger than vacuum cleaners.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Toy robots can speak three different languages.

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information
options.FALSE: If the statement contradicts the information
options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

questions.explanation: The passage only mentions that they 'teach children simple English words'. It does not say if they can speak three languages.

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: What do helper robots remind older people to take?
questions.explanation: The fourth paragraph states: '...and remind older people to take their medicine.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Toy robots are great _________ for kids.
questions.explanation: The fifth paragraph states: 'They are great friends for kids.'

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài đạt điểm cao (A1-A2)

1. Tìm từ khóa giống hệt trong câu hỏi: Ở trình độ này, các câu hỏi không dùng từ đồng nghĩa phức tạp. Hãy khoanh tròn từ khóa chính (ví dụ: 'Japan', 'medicine', 'vacuum cleaner') và tìm đúng từ đó trong bài đọc.

2. Lưu ý số lượng từ giới hạn: Khi làm câu hỏi điền từ (Q9 và Q10), hãy chú ý yêu cầu 'NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS'. Chỉ lấy từ vựng trực tiếp có trong bài đọc, không tự ý đổi dạng từ (ví dụ: giữ nguyên 'medicine').

3. Phân biệt False và Not Given: Nếu thông tin trong bài ngược lại hoàn toàn với câu hỏi, chọn FALSE. Nếu câu hỏi nhắc đến một chi tiết không hề có trong bài (ví dụ: 'three languages' ở Q8), chọn NOT GIVEN.
